programme.title: Newscast
programme.desc: Newsy chat and analysis from Adam Fleming, Laura Kuenssberg, Chris Mason and guests.

programme.title: Weather for the Week Ahead
programme.desc: Detailed weather forecast.

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: BBC One joins the BBC's rolling news channel for a night of news.

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: The latest news, sport, business and weather from the BBC's Breakfast team.

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones present a lively mix of topical content and expert advice.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A semi in Mottingham in London, a detached house in Littleover in Derby and a terrace in Dover in Kent are all renovated to a very high standard. The difference is the time taken to complete the work, which run from under a week to more than two and a half years. Martin, Dion and Martel keep an eye on progress.
programme.category: Home & Garden, Bouwen en verbouwen, Documentaire

programme.title: Murder, Mystery & My Family: Case Closed?
programme.desc: Barristers Sasha and Jeremy look back at the case of a mother accused of murdering her family at the height of a poison panic in Victorian Britain.

programme.title: Island Medics
programme.desc: Behind the scenes at the UK's most remote hospital, the Gilbert Bain in Shetland.

programme.title: Bargain Hunt: Animals Special
programme.desc: Eric Knowles enters the animal kingdom for a Bargain Hunt special looking at some of the most interesting animal-related items bought by the red and blue teams. It can be a jungle out there, so Eric offers advice on how to spot a bargain. He also heads to Peterborough to discover an extraordinary collection of horseshoes.

programme.title: BBC News at One
programme.desc: The latest national and international news from the BBC.

programme.title: Regional News and Weather
programme.desc: The latest news, sport and weather.

programme.title: WPC 56
programme.desc: Gina's life is put in mortal danger when an attempt to lure the killer takes a deadly twist. Meanwhile, Jack makes a horrifying discovery about the killer's past, but can he get to Gina in time?

programme.title: Garden Rescue: Top of the Plots
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and the Rich brothers, David and Harry, remember some of their favourite exotic garden makeovers. These are the spaces that take influence from far-flung countries all over the world, from Malaysia to Zambia, and they include a Caribbean garden full of tropical planting, an Eastern paradise with a centre-piece moon gate, a Japanese garden complete with a yoga deck, and a tiny backyard transformed into an Indian Mughal Empire-influenced garden. Charlie and the boys also offer plenty of fresh gardening tips, including how to bring exotic planting into your garden and how to look after bamboo properly.
programme.category: Home & Garden, Bouwen en verbouwen, Reality

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton helps a couple from Newcastle who love their Lake District holidays so much they want to make the area their permanent home. With a budget of £750,000, they are looking for a property with fabulous views and enough space for visiting family. Alistair also takes to the water when he meets a passionate boat builder who restored what is thought to be the oldest passenger ferry in the world.
programme.category: Home & Garden, Bouwen en verbouwen, Business & Financial

programme.title: My Unique B&B
programme.desc: Simon and his team are in the stunning Somerset levels, working with Vicki and Bryan. The couple dreams of spending less time at work and more time with their young family by turning a corner of their field into a unique B&B. They have a space-age geodome but no idea of how to transform it into a welcoming rental. Simon and his team are full of ideas, including a large wooden statement bed and set of bunks, as well as a botanical theme to bring warmth and colour to the large space. It's a big and physically challenging job, but with help from friends and family, the team pull off an extraordinary transformation.

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: It's an Antiques Boat Trip as Charlie and Izzie set sail on the Solent for an Isle of Wight antiques hunt. Both uncover silver goodies, but who'll take gold at the final auction?

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: The quiz in which contestants try to score as few points as possible by plumbing the depths of their general knowledge to come up with the answers no-one else could think of. Presented by Alexander Armstrong and co-host Richard Osman.

programme.title: BBC News at Six
programme.desc: The latest national and international news stories from the BBC News team, followed by weather.

programme.title: Regional News and Weather
programme.desc: The latest news, sport and weather.

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Ronan Keating bring you the stories that matter from across the country.

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Rob Brydon, Lee Mack and David Mitchell return for an episode of previously unseen material from the hit comedy panel show. Over the course of the show, a stellar cast of celebrity guests reveal amazing stories about themselves. But are they telling the truth or are they making it all up? Among the guests joining Lee and David are Clare Balding, Liz Bonnin, Jo Brand, Asim Chaudhry, Victoria Coren Mitchell, Greg Davies, Simon Day, Greg James, Guz Khan, Gabby Logan, Chris McCausland, Stephen Merchant, Kiri Pritchard-McLean, Angela Scanlon, John Simm, Dr Ranj Singh, Henning Wehn, Claudia Winkleman and Lucy Worsley.

programme.title: Not Going Out
programme.desc: Sitcom. To teach the children to be more responsible, Lee and Lucy get a family pet.

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Billy and Rocky go head-to-head, Gray lashes out at his boss, and Kheerat is concerned for Suki.

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: When a bride-to-be is killed on the last night of her hen party, DI Goodman tries to unravel a seemingly impossible murder. Meanwhile Camille has a big decision to make.

programme.title: BBC News at Ten
programme.desc: The latest national and international news, with reports from BBC correspondents worldwide.

programme.title: Regional News and Weather
programme.desc: The latest news, sport and weather.

programme.title: A Simple Favour
programme.desc: Topsy-turvy thriller about a friendship between two mothers which outs some deeply buried secrets. While popular vlogger Stephanie is the perfect parent, her shy persona is challenged by force of nature Emily - a PR director in the fashion industry who lives a secretive lifestyle. As their children grow closer, so do the two mums, but the pair's fledgling bond comes under threat when Emily does not return home after a work emergency.

programme.title: Weather for the Week Ahead
programme.desc: Detailed weather forecast.

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: BBC One joins the BBC's rolling news channel for a night of news.

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: The latest news, sport, business and weather from the BBC's Breakfast team.

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Live
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt and chefs Freddy Bird and Meera Sodha are joined by special guest Miles Jupp. There are great moments from the BBC food archive, including clips from Rick Stein, Keith Floyd, Nigella Lawson and Mary Berry, and drinks expert Helen McGinn joins via video link to pick the wines to go with the studio dishes.

programme.title: Classic Mary Berry
programme.desc: Soothing and much-loved home comfort delights, from the very simple to the extremely decadent. Mary indulges her love of comfort food with homely recipes to warm the heart, including eggs benedict florentine, fall-off-the-bone slow-cooked lamb shanks and deliciously rich chocolate truffle pots. She invites Swedish fire-chef Niklas Esktedt to share the joys of caveman cooking with her, and finds herself outside by an open fire enjoying his inventive venison meatballs with blackened celeriac and mouthwatering Swedish donuts.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A large terraced house in Earlsfield in London, ancient woodland in Eythorne in Kent and a building housing six flats in Tonypandy in Wales are all sold under the hammer. Martin, Dion and Martel meet the new owners and hear their plans, which range from creating a family home for themselves to extending a school and renovating for the rental market.
programme.category: Home & Garden, Bouwen en verbouwen, Documentaire

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: The latest national and international news from the BBC.

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Natasha Raskin Sharp and the team are in Ardingly at the South of England Showground. The reds and blues battle it out around the fair with experts Tim Weeks and Caroline Hawley. Meanwhile, Natasha shows off a rather quirky find with a hidden surprise.

programme.title: Rugby League Challenge Cup Final: St Helens v Castleford
programme.desc: Mark Chapman introduces live coverage as St Helens bid to make it lucky number 13 in the Challenge Cup final against Castleford Tigers. Twelve-time winners Saints have a rich Challenge Cup pedigree, but the reigning Super League champions have not won the showpiece final since 2008. Victory here would cap a 2021 double for Saints after their women's team beat York City Knights earlier in the year at the Leigh Sports Village. Castleford's Challenge Cup wait has been even longer - they last won the tournament in 1986 - but they will be desperate to see off the Saints to give Daryl Powell the perfect send-off. Head coach Powell is leaving the Tigers at the end of the season. Will an emotionally charged afternoon end with celebrations for Castleford?

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: The latest national and international news from the BBC.

programme.title: Regional News and Weather
programme.desc: The latest news, sport and weather.

programme.title: The Hit List Celebrity Special
programme.desc: Marvin and Rochelle Humes host this very special celebrity episode. Teaming up to play are comedian Susan Calman and Radio 1 DJ Melvin Odoom, former footballer John Barnes and Radio 1 DJ Scott Mills and football pundit and presenter Chris Kamara and Capital Radio DJ Vick Hope. The pressure is on to name as many hit songs and artists as possible to win £10,000 for their chosen charities, especially for the DJs!

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: A celebrity sport edition of the general knowledge quiz in which four teams try to come up with the answers that no-one else could think of. Presented by Alexander Armstrong and co-host Richard Osman, with contestants Tanni Grey-Thompson, David Wilkie, Matthew Hoggard, Graeme Swann, Jo Pavey, Ade Adepitan, Helen Glover and Heather Stanning.

programme.title: The Wall
programme.desc: Olivia and Matt, a married couple from Worcester, face questions and big decisions as they bid to win money to help get a bigger house, now they have a baby daughter. But in a show with so many twists and turns, anything could happen.

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Fenisha makes the ultimate choice to follow her heart, and David is distraught to realise he's destroyed his family.

programme.title: Dunkirk
programme.desc: An epic wartime action thriller written, directed and produced by Christopher Nolan. Hundreds of thousands of British and Allied troops find themselves surrounded by enemy forces in Dunkirk, northern France, during World War II. They face an impossible situation, trapped on the beach as the German troops close in. A quickly assembled British fleet leads the legendary evacuation of over 300,000 Allied military forces.

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: The latest national and international news from the BBC.

programme.title: Young Guns
programme.desc: Offbeat western set in 1870s New Mexico. Six young outlaws led by Billy the Kid (Emilio Estevez) ride out to avenge the death of their mentor, a British rancher murdered by a cattle baron. The tearaways are deputised and told to bring the guilty men back alive, but Billy has other plans. The terrific supporting cast includes Kiefer Sutherland, Charlie Sheen, Jack Palance and Terence Stamp.

programme.title: Weather for the Week Ahead
programme.desc: Detailed weather forecast.

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: BBC One joins the BBC's rolling news channel for a night of news.

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: The latest news, sport, business and weather from the BBC's Breakfast team.

programme.title: The Andrew Marr Show
programme.desc: Andrew Marr is joined by Sajid Javid MP, health secretary, and Jonathan Ashworth MP, shadow health secretary. With news review by Victoria Newton, editor-in-chief of The Sun, and Vicki Young, BBC deputy political editor.
programme.category: Talkshow, Actualiteitenprogramma's, Nieuws, Interview, Actualiteit, Podiumkunsten

programme.title: Politics England
programme.desc: The latest political news, interviews and debate.

programme.title: Sunday Morning Live
programme.desc: Topical magazine show with Sean Fletcher and Nina Wadia covering the week's talking points and exploring the ethical and religious issues of the day. Alongside lively chat and special guests, the show also shines a light on thought-provoking and inspiring stories from around the UK.

programme.title: Wanted Down Under
programme.desc: The Onaivi family are well and truly at odds when to comes to where they want to spend their lives. Mum Krystyn wants to give her children the sunshine lifestyle she loved as a child by moving to Australia, but husband Joseph is more than happy with his life in the UK. A trial week in Perth gives them the chance to look at the homes, jobs and lifestyle on offer, but will it give them answers or drive them further apart?

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Eric Knowles and the team are on the Welsh island of Anglesey. The reds and blues go head to head at the antiques fair with experts Thomas Plant and Caroline Hawley. The teams have only one hour to buy three items, to sell for profit at auction. But who will make the most profit and win the show?

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: The latest national and international news from the BBC.

programme.title: Songs of Praise: Arundel Cathedral
programme.desc: Katherine Jenkins is in Arundel in West Sussex, home to the Dukes and Duchesses of Norfolk. She visits the Roman Catholic cathedral built by the fifteenth duke as a statement of his faith, and she explores the stunning castle gardens. Radzi Chinyanganya is invited to the Convent of the Poor Clares in Arundel to hear how they came to top the charts with their classical album, and we find out about Wheels for the World, a Christian project transforming lives by restoring wheelchairs to send to developing countries. Also, stand by fans of gospel music! It's the launch of the BBC Songs of Praise Gospel Singer of the Year competition. Details of how to enter, terms and conditions and privacy notice can be found on the website bbc.co.uk/songofpraise. The deadline for entries is midnight on 15 August 2021.

programme.title: Cricket: England v Pakistan T20 Live
programme.desc: Isa Guha introduces live coverage from Headingley as England and Pakistan meet in the second of a three-match Twenty20 series. With August's Test series against India looming large, the England squad will be anxious for a strong showing in Leeds.

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and the Rich brothers compete to design a £6,000 Italian garden for a couple in Fareham. Their creative skills are tested to the limit as they try to turn an uninspiring grass patch into a Mediterranean idyll.
programme.category: Home & Garden, Bouwen en verbouwen, Reality

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: The latest national and international news from the BBC.

programme.title: Regional News and Weather
programme.desc: The latest news, sport and weather.

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Anita Rani and Tom Heap head to Yorkshire's Flamborough Head. Anita visits England's largest onshore seabird colony at Bempton Cliffs, where she helps to track breeding numbers and witnesses a very special arrival as a rare albatross pays a visit. Below the cliffs, Tom explores some of the coast's chalk caves, discovering the rare life they support, and Tom and Anita are joined by local youngsters as they go on a rockpool safari to take stock of the marine life washed up at low tide. Charlotte Smith investigates the environmental impact that increases in offshore windfarms are having both on land and at sea. Adam Henson visits an project that gives teenagers a step on to the farming ladder, and in the third of our four-part series with explorer Dwyane Fields, our four youngsters overcome their fear of heights, getting to grips with climbing a rockface - but things take an unexpected turn when one of the team has to leave the challenge early.
programme.category: Natuur en milieu, Landbouw, Dieren, Reizen, Outdoor, Nieuws

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: The team make a second visit to Kenilworth Castle in the Midlands, where finds include some early Meccano toys, a Special Ops radio set used behind the lines in WWII and a set of nursing medals that span three global conflicts. Fiona Bruce looks into the art of silhouette-making as an artist cuts her own portrait.

programme.title: Baptiste
programme.desc: Julien Baptiste travels to Hungary to help the British ambassador, Emma Chambers, find her missing family. When a body is found, the pressureis on to save Emma's two sons.

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: The latest national and international news from the BBC.

programme.title: Regional News and Weather
programme.desc: The latest news, sport and weather.

programme.title: The Limehouse Golem
programme.desc: After a series of violent murders scandalises Victorian London, the Limehouse Golem strikes fear into the hearts of the public, whilst also capturing people's imagination. Into this melting pot is thrust the well-meaning Inspector Kildare, who has never been given a murder case before. He knows he has been set up to fail but soon uncovers a lead that implicates Karl Marx, George Gissing and the popular music hall star Dan Leno, and follows the trail, wherever it may lead, through the seediest and most intriguing parts of London.

programme.title: The Wall
programme.desc: Olivia and Matt, a married couple from Worcester, face questions and big decisions as they bid to win money to help get a bigger house, now they have a baby daughter. But in a show with so many twists and turns, anything could happen.

programme.title: Weather for the Week Ahead
programme.desc: Detailed weather forecast.

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: BBC One joins the BBC's rolling news channel for a night of news.

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: The latest news, sport, business and weather from the BBC's Breakfast team.

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones present a lively mix of topical content and expert advice.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A three-bedroom semi on a corner plot in Romford in Essex, a large four bedroom terrace with period features in Liverpool and a former bank in Nuneaton in Warwickshire are all sold under the hammer. All the new owners bought at auction as an investment, but did any of them manage to make any money?
programme.category: Home & Garden, Bouwen en verbouwen, Documentaire

programme.title: Murder, Mystery and My Family: Case Closed?
programme.desc: The barristers revisit their inquiry of an infamous Victorian murder case, where a young American woman was charged with poisoning her husband in Liverpool in 1889.

programme.title: Island Medics
programme.desc: Behind the scenes at the UK's most remote hospital, the Gilbert Bain in Shetland, where the team of doctors and nurses work alongside the islands' GPs, paramedics, coastguard and the fire and rescue service to provide emergency and medical care to Shetland's residents and offshore workers, fishermen and tourists around the clock, whatever the weather. There are serious concerns for a baby boy who arrives in A&E feeling very unwell. The team needs to make a quick decision about whether they can help him, or whether he requires specialist care 225 miles away on the Scottish mainland. Occupational therapist Marc Beswick returns to long-term patient Summer. The four-year-old has a condition that means it's hard for her to move her arms and legs. Marc has been working on her electric wheelchair, making modifications to make sure she can develop some independence. And Dr Lalla takes a break from A&E on a family fishing trip with nurse Thelma Irvine.

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Charlie Ross and experts Thomas Forrester and Stephanie Connell go antique-hunting in Brackley, Northamptonshire, before heading to Stratford-upon-Avon for today's auction. Charlie seeks illumination about the life of Victorian designer Charles Kempe, famous for his stained glass.

programme.title: BBC News at One
programme.desc: The latest national and international news from the BBC.

programme.title: Regional News and Weather
programme.desc: The latest news, sport and weather.

programme.title: WPC 56
programme.desc: The travelling fair comes to Brinford, and the search for a runaway girl draws WPC Gina Dawson into a feud between the fair workers and a gang of teddy boys. Back at the station she struggles to remain professional when Jack makes a shock announcement. Meanwhile, Fenton clashes with the new DI when a dead body is discovered, but is there more to the mystery than either of them realise?

programme.title: Garden Rescue: Top of the Plots
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and the Rich brothers, David and Harry, take a look at some of their favourite transformational garden rescues. A concrete back yard is turned into a garden paradise complete with poppy meadow, a new build back garden becomes the ultimate outdoor party area complete with bar, and a garden on a 45-degree slope is transformed with sheets of steel. Charlie and the boys also offer fresh tips, including the best way to disguise ugly sights in your garden with clever planting, and Charlie creates the perfect hanging basket.
programme.category: Home & Garden, Bouwen en verbouwen, Reality

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: In England's smallest county, Rutland, a couple from Buckinghamshire with a budget of £500,000 ask Steve Brown and the team to find them a detached property with a garden big enough for a vegetable patch. Continuing the horticultural theme, Steve visits Barnsdale Gardens to see for himself the legacy left by one of the country's best loved gardeners, the late Geoff Hamilton.
programme.category: Home & Garden, Bouwen en verbouwen, Business & Financial

programme.title: My Unique B&B
programme.desc: Simon and the team are in the dramatic Black Mountains of Wales with Alex and Laura. The couple have a busy life with a craft cider business and a young family, and now they want to expand their enterprise to include unique accommodation for paying guests. They have already started to build a tiny house on wheels but want the team to help fulfil its potential. Simon and the team set to work designing and building stunning indoor and outdoor living areas, including a bath with an incredible view.

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: The start of a brand new Road Trip with a fresh-faced expert making a debut appearance. Old hand Angus Ashworth is showing 'new boy' Steven Moore the ropes in the first of a five leg adventure. Steven is a former dealer, auctioneer and museum curator and has been valuing antiques on the telly for years. However, has he got what it takes to beat veteran Tripper Angus when it comes to finding valuable antiques? Their 1960 Hillman Super Minx fires up in the Scottish Borders, and purrs towards an auction in the Lake District at Crooklands, near Kendal. As well as lots of top treasure seeking, today's episode also includes the story of the country's greatest archers and a trip to a part of Scotland that's slowly revealing its secrets to archaeologists.

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: The quiz in which contestants try to score as few points as possible by plumbing the depths of their general knowledge to come up with the answers no-one else could think of. Presented by Alexander Armstrong and co-host Richard Osman.

programme.title: BBC News at Six
programme.desc: The latest national and international news stories from the BBC News team, followed by weather.

programme.title: Regional News and Weather
programme.desc: The latest news, sport and weather.

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Jermaine Jenas bring you the stories that matter from across the country.

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Tony and Nayna in Liverpool love being outdoors, but their new build garden is uninspiring. It's just grass with some planting, and the couple have run out of ideas. They would like a Caribbean theme with a modern twist, and Tony dreams of a centre-piece water feature. It must have a multi-functional pergola for entertaining and for Tony to use his punchbag, and they want plenty of tropical planting. Charlie and Lee may need to punch above their weight to get the job done with a budget of £4,500.
programme.category: Home & Garden, Bouwen en verbouwen, Reality

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Callum becomes desperate when Ben refuses to listen to his pleas. Tiffany pushes Bernie to get an answer, and Lola comes to terms with her new reality.

programme.title: The Dark Side of Horse Racing - Panorama
programme.desc: Horse racing is one of the most popular and profitable sports, a £5 billion industry in the UK and Ireland followed by millions. Panorama reporter Darragh MacIntyre investigates what can happen to racehorses when their careers end. The industry says that racing is now safer than ever, that the number of deaths on the track is falling and that the animals are looked after in retirement. Panorama discovers that off the track, many horses suffer career-ending injuries, and rather than being rehabilitated or retrained for life outside the sport, race horses that have been owned and trained by some of the biggest names in the industry have been put down, some meeting grisly deaths.

programme.title: Gold Rush: Our Race to Olympic Glory
programme.desc: After triumphing in Beijing, Britain's athletes have four years to topple the sporting superpowers at London 2012, as the organisers face a sceptical press and public.

programme.title: BBC News at Ten
programme.desc: The latest national and international news, with reports from BBC correspondents worldwide.

programme.title: Regional News and Weather
programme.desc: The latest news, sport and weather.

programme.title: Helen Glover: The Mother of All Comebacks
programme.desc: After winning gold in Rio 2016, double Olympic champion Helen Glover decided to hang up the oars and step away from rowing to start a family. Helen had three children together with husband Steve Backshall, but the Covid-19 pandemic and ensuing lockdown brought on a change in perspective. Despite not having stepped foot in a boat since Rio, Helen decided to take a shot at a third Olympic gold. This documentary follows Helen's journey back into racing every step of the way, from regaining her fitness during the first lockdown in early 2020 to her selection for Team GB. It provides a powerful insight into what it takes to balance new motherhood with elite sporting performance, exploring the highs and lows of the road to Tokyo through the lens of those closest to her.

programme.title: Have I Got a Bit More News for You
programme.desc: Team captains Paul Merton and Ian Hislop are joined by guest host Steph McGovern and panellists Miles Jupp and Baroness Warsi, all from the worlds of comedy, journalism and current affairs.

programme.title: Eat Well for Less?
programme.desc: Gregg and Chris head to Cardiff to meet a family who are stuck in a food rut due to their seven-year-old son's serious food allergies.

programme.title: Weather for the Week Ahead
programme.desc: Detailed weather forecast.

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: BBC One joins the BBC's rolling news channel for a night of news.

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: The latest news, sport, business and weather from the BBC's Breakfast team.

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones present a lively mix of topical content and expert advice.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A terraced house in Wigston in Leicestershire, a flat in Swanscombe in Kent and a piece of land in Bolton in Lancashire are sold under the hammer. All the developers have ambitious plans for their projects, but will everything go according to those plans?
programme.category: Home & Garden, Bouwen en verbouwen, Documentaire

programme.title: Murder, Mystery and My Family: Case Closed?
programme.desc: The barristers revisit their investigation into the poisoning of a care home resident in Nottingham in 1935, for which the victim's nurse was convicted and hanged.

programme.title: Island Medics
programme.desc: In this episode, paramedics and the coastguard rescue team help a six foot nine inch Norwegian nicknamed Tiny off his tall ship. The sailor was on his way to the Shetland Folk Festival, but having a bad ankle fracture means he'll spend the night in hospital instead. Nurse Amanda treats an old school friend, fisherman Neil, who was also hoping to make it to the Folk Festival. He arrives in A&E with excruciating backpain. Specialist paramedic Emma Stevenson takes time off to play her fiddle at one of the festival's more unusual venues.

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: The team pays a visit to the Highland Centre in Edinburgh, where presenter Natasha Raskin Sharp is joined by experts Ben Cooper and Tim Weeks to help the reds and blues track down items that they hope will make them a profit at auction. Meanwhile, Natasha pays a visit to the Jupiter Artland sculpture park in Edinburgh.

programme.title: BBC News at One
programme.desc: The latest national and international news from the BBC.

programme.title: Regional News and Weather
programme.desc: The latest news, sport and weather.

programme.title: WPC 56
programme.desc: Max and Fenton investigate a flood of counterfeit notes; is it too much of a coincidence that pickpocket Brody's luck has changed? The outcome of the boxing match leaves everyone reeling as Lenny Powell shows his true colours. When the murder investigation stalls, Max puts Gina forward for a risky assignment, but can she pull it off?

programme.title: Garden Rescue: Top of the Plots
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and the Rich brothers, David and Harry, look back at some of their favourite inspirational garden rescues, the ones that drew inspiration from special memories. This includes a garden transformation with memories of a honeymoon in the south of France, a New York skyline-inspired garden designed by Arit Anderson, a Moroccan paradise and a space inspired by the Lost Gardens of Heligan. There's also plenty of fresh gardening advice from Charlie and the boys, including how to combat air pollution with climbing plants and the best way to plant a window box.
programme.category: Home & Garden, Bouwen en verbouwen, Reality

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Sonali Shah is in Devon, accompanying a married couple from Berkshire with an impressive budget of £1,200,000. On today's property shopping list is a five-bedroom detached property with land where their four children can enjoy the great outdoors. Away from the house hunt, Sonali visits a farm where she discovers that flowers are not the only way to her heart. Edible flowers are too.
programme.category: Home & Garden, Bouwen en verbouwen, Business & Financial

programme.title: My Unique B&B
programme.desc: In the Forest of Dean, Simon and the team meet semi-retired couple Becky and David. Now that their children have flown the nest, they're keen for a new adventure and want to set up a small glamping business in their peaceful and picturesque garden. They've called in Simon and his team to help them convert their old drab yurt into an a colourful and inviting bijou B&B, complete with outdoor kitchen and bespoke firepit and seating.

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: It's the second leg of the trip and our two chums, Steven Moore and Angus Ashworth, plus their shiny, white Hillman Super Minx called 'Betty', are shopping their way along the byways of Scotland. Yorkshireman Angus bags something aromatic and coffee related, while Geordie new boy Steven plumps for a little treasure from his fellow tripper's back yard in Yorkshire. But will their plans pay off at the auction? On the way, we visit Scotland's largest lowland loch to find out about a fascinating former resident who made a dramatic escape from imprisonment and also take a crash course in chemistry. All before heading to the sale room at Kinbuck.

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: The quiz in which contestants try to score as few points as possible by plumbing the depths of their general knowledge to come up with the answers no-one else could think of. Presented by Alexander Armstrong and co-host Richard Osman.

programme.title: BBC News at Six
programme.desc: The latest national and international news stories from the BBC News team, followed by weather.

programme.title: Regional News and Weather
programme.desc: The latest news, sport and weather.

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Jermaine Jenas bring you the stories that matter from across the country.

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: The Panesars try to stick together for Jags's funeral, and Callum fears for Ben.

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Dom is shocked when he discovers Carole's secret, Hanssen is torn as to how to handle Abs's secret, and can Josh prove to Ange that he's up to the role of being a father?

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Rob Brydon is back in the host's chair with Lee Mack and David Mitchell returning as the team captains. Sorting the fact from the fiction this week are guests Les Dennis, Richard Osman, Alice Levine and Lou Sanders.

programme.title: Uprising
programme.desc: In the early hours of 18 January 1981, in a house in south London, a birthday party ended in a fire. Thirteen young black British people died. The fire and its aftermath would ignite an uprising by the black British community. This film tells the stories of the young people who were at the party and the events that led up to it.

programme.title: BBC News at Ten
programme.desc: The latest national and international news, with reports from BBC correspondents worldwide.

programme.title: Regional News and Weather
programme.desc: The latest news, sport and weather.

programme.title: Flat Out Fabulous
programme.desc: Whinnie and Tyler meet seven housemates who are desperate to give their digs a new lease of life. Can Whinnie pull off a Roaring Twenties cinema theme for the cost of their big night out?

programme.title: Flat Out Fabulous
programme.desc: Cardiff calls, and Whinnie's not the only queen in town. She and Tyler meet three housemates: Leon, Callum and Rhys - aka Leonce - Malady Masterwort and Just Becky, drag queens who live together. The trio love living in Cardiff for its great nightlife and the hustle and bustle of living in the capital. They are big personalities, but their living space does not represent them, so they've called in the Flat Out Fabulous duo to zhoosh up their space. Digging deep into their spending habits, it turns out our drag queens don't go on big nights out - they have big weekends. So with a hefty budget in tow, Whinnie comes up with a backstage boutique-inspired living room. Whinnie and Tyler set them the task of silver leafing the fireplace, upcycling an old cabinet and turning a globe into a mirror ball. Can she pull it off with her most demanding of households yet?

programme.title: Fast Food Face-Off
programme.desc: Comedian Josh Jones pits Kristian Nairn and Gemma Bradley against each other in a competition to find the best takeaway in town.

programme.title: Bates Motel
programme.desc: Caleb remains chained in the basement as Chick starts to make himself useful in the Bates house. Madeleine arrives to apologise for the disastrous dinner and explains that things are strained between her and Sam.

programme.title: Bates Motel
programme.desc: As Chick disposes of Caleb's body, Norman is visited by Sheriff Jane Greene who questions him about Jim Blackwell. Blackwell has skipped bail, and the motel's address has been found amongst his possessions.

programme.title: Weather for the Week Ahead
programme.desc: Detailed weather forecast.

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: BBC One joins the BBC's rolling news channel for a night of news.

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: The latest news, sport, business and weather from the BBC's Breakfast team.

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones present a lively mix of topical content and expert advice.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts and Lucy Alexander visit a terraced property in Devon, and revisit a flat in London and a bungalow in North Lanarkshire. All of these properties went to auction and we'll find out who bought them and what they paid when they went under the hammer.
programme.category: Home & Garden, Bouwen en verbouwen, Documentaire

programme.title: Murder, Mystery and My Family: Case Closed?
programme.desc: The barristers revisit their investigation of an infamous case from 1856 that inspired Thomas Hardy's novel Tess of the D'Urbervilles.

programme.title: Island Medics
programme.desc: Behind the scenes at the UK's most remote hospital, the Gilbert Bain in Shetland. In this episode, an oil rig worker from Tyne and Wear is rushed to A&E with a suspected stroke.

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Eric Knowles and the teams are bargain hunting at an antiques centre in Cheshire, and we are spicing things up with some changes to the rules. The red and blues with the help of experts Ben Cooper and Richard Madley still have one hour to find three items, but now one of their items must cost at least £75 and they must also buy an item of Eric's choosing. Eric goes to Keele University to learn about a special ingredient that improved the manufacture of English porcelain.

programme.title: BBC News at One
programme.desc: The latest national and international news from the BBC.

programme.title: Regional News and Weather
programme.desc: The latest news, sport and weather.

programme.title: WPC 56
programme.desc: An undercover operation for Fenton threatens to expose a secret that could ruin Inspector Briggs. Susie takes matters into her own hands when Tommy is bullied by the other officers, and Max finally gets his witness in the murder investigation; now he just needs her to talk. At the Sapphire Club, Lenny taunts Chris and Gina struggles with her growing feelings for him. Meanwhile, Rosie and Fenton both make choices that will cost them dearly.

programme.title: Garden Rescue: Top of the Plots
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and the Rich brothers, David and Harry, look back at some of their favourite eco-garden transformations. There's a garden that features a hedgehog highway for rescued hedgehogs, Arit builds a garden to attract bees, taking her design cues from beehives, and a battered old shed is transformed using recycled materials. There are also plenty of brilliant new gardening tips from Charlie and the boys, including an inventive way with composters and the best ways to encourage bees into your garden.
programme.category: Home & Garden, Bouwen en verbouwen, Reality

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: A couple from Bedfordshire with a budget of £450,000 need Ginny Buckley's help in Norfolk to find them the first house they are buying together. They would like a character property with a good-sized garden for some new canine companions. Ginny also heads to Great Yarmouth, where she straps in for a white-knuckle ride on one of the country's last remaining wooden rollercoasters.
programme.category: Home & Garden, Bouwen en verbouwen, Business & Financial

programme.title: My Unique B&B
programme.desc: Mel and Tom live in the oldest house in their North Somerset village of Wrington. They are both writers, but they admit they don't have any creativity when it comes to interior design. They want to turn their attic room into a quirky B&B rental suite with a writer's retreat theme. Simon and Ian build a bespoke floor-to-ceiling bookcase with a hidden door, while designer Verity steps in to help inject some personality into the bedroom.

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: It's the halfway point of our treasure trip, and experts Angus Ashworth and Steven Moore are still having fun in bonnie Scotland. Today they take the low road which is just as well considering their 1960s Hillman Minx has serious issues with hills! Auctioneer Angus, a Yorkshireman, thinks his Scottish name might somehow help him in the haggle. While Steven, a Geordie and former auctioneer, dealer and curator - prefers to subtly roll his 'R's... On the way to a saleroom back below the border in Clitheroe, they discover the wellspring of water polo and enjoy a very soggy Highland Games. Expect swimming costumes and caber tossing.

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: The quiz in which contestants try to score as few points as possible by plumbing the depths of their general knowledge to come up with the answers no-one else could think of. Presented by Alexander Armstrong and co-host Richard Osman.

programme.title: BBC News at Six
programme.desc: The latest national and international news stories from the BBC News team, followed by weather.

programme.title: Regional News and Weather
programme.desc: The latest news, sport and weather.

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Jermaine Jenas bring you the stories that matter from across the country.

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: Nigel Havers welcomes three more sellers to the Bidding Room, where they try to sell items to professional dealers who are on the lookout for interesting pieces, with help from expert Simon Bower. First through the doors is Annalisa, with a unique piece of farming history. Will the dealers work out what it is? Also facing the dealers are mother and daughters Jennifer, Ann and Gina, with a fascinating tribal mask bought from their local school. Simon helps them work out where it came from before they face the dealers. Using their new knowledge, can they strike a good deal? Last to try her luck with our buyers is Chrissy, with a charming family heirloom: a toy horse and cart that she hopes will see her galloping off with a tidy profit. Today's dealers are Adi, Ian, James, Jane and Tash. Will any of them match - or even top - the valuation placed on each of the items?

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Jay Blades and the team tackle a rare historic painting, a vintage sewing machine, a Beatles souvenir from the Swinging Sixties and a splintered stained-glass window.

programme.title: Uprising
programme.desc: The story of the aftermath of the New Cross fire and the run up to the Black People's Day of Action. As news spread about the fire at 439 New Cross Road, the scale of the tragedy overwhelmed the local community. Amid uncertainty about whether the fire had been caused by a racist firebomb attack, anger mounted at the police investigation and the seeming indifference of the press and the government to the loss of so many black lives. The Black People's Day of Action, a mass demonstration, was organised to bring the tragedy to the attention of the nation.

programme.title: BBC News at Ten
programme.desc: The latest national and international news, with reports from BBC correspondents worldwide.

programme.title: Regional News and Weather
programme.desc: The latest news, sport and weather.

programme.title: Jade Jones: Fighting for Gold
programme.desc: An intimate portrait of double Olympic taekwondo gold medalist Jade Jones as she prepares to try to make history by becoming the first British woman to win gold medals in three successive games. The film offers a highly personal insight into the life of the tough, competitive and uncompromising girl from Flint. Jade, her friends, her family and her trainers reveal how a troubled youth dominated by shoplifting and problems at school was transformed through her love of sport. Nothing has come easy for her - she even had to raise money to pay for training by cockle fishing in the mud flats near her home and by selling scrap metal with her dad. Now she stands on the brink of unprecedented sporting success, and missing out on a third gold in Japan is simply not an option.

programme.title: Sir Chris Hoy's Secret World of Japanese Bicycle Racing
programme.desc: Olympic icon Sir Chris Hoy heads to Japan, host of the 2021 Tokyo Games, to explore the phenomenon of the keirin, the most extreme and exciting event in track cycling. A test of controlled pacing and then a tactical fight to the line, keirin is a sport in its own right in its country of origin and is steeped in the history and rituals that make Japanese culture so unique. Attending keirin school is a once-in-a-lifetime opportunity for international riders, and back in 2005, multiple Olympic gold medallist Sir Chris was one of the first Britons to be invited to savour the ultimate experience in the career of any track cyclist. He now makes a nostalgic return.

programme.title: Gold Rush: Our Race to Olympic Glory
programme.desc: After triumphing in Beijing, Britain's athletes have four years to topple the sporting superpowers at London 2012, as the organisers face a sceptical press and public.

programme.title: Weather for the Week Ahead
programme.desc: Detailed weather forecast.

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: BBC One joins the BBC's rolling news channel for a night of news.

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: The latest news, sport, business and weather from the BBC's Breakfast team.

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones present a lively mix of topical content and expert advice.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts and Lucy Alexander visit a semi-detached house on the outskirts of Cardiff, a fantastic property in Kent and a property in Shropshire. All of these properties have been sold at auction and we'll find out who bought them and what they paid for them when they went under the hammer.
programme.category: Home & Garden, Bouwen en verbouwen, Documentaire

programme.title: Murder, Mystery and My Family: Case Closed?
programme.desc: The barristers revisit their investigation into a case of starvation and neglect that saw a husband stand trial for the murder of his wife in 1877.

programme.title: Island Medics
programme.desc: Behind the scenes at the UK's most remote hospital, the Gilbert Bain in Shetland.

programme.title: Bargain Hunt: Homes and Interiors Special
programme.desc: Natasha Raskin Sharp looks back at some decorative buys from past episodes, presenting the show from a beautiful country manor house in Scotland. Charlie Ross learns all about the craft of Welsh blankets, and Eric Knowles visits Liverpool's St Georges Hall to admire its incredible interior design.

programme.title: BBC News at One
programme.desc: The latest national and international news from the BBC.

programme.title: Regional News and Weather
programme.desc: The latest news, sport and weather.

programme.title: WPC 56
programme.desc: Coulson's sexual advances on Gina take a sinister turn, Fenton's corruption is exposed and Swift becomes obsessed with Susie's new beau. Max finally makes an arrest for the murders of Rosie and Daniel, but has he got the right man? Gina risks jeopardising everything when trouble at work pushes her towards Chris.

programme.title: Garden Rescue: Top of the Plots
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and the Rich brothers, David and Harry, look back at some of the brothers' favourite garden rescues. There are two gardens in Manchester that became one space complete with a Scandinavian influence, a tiny back-garden that was turned into a wildlife paradise that maximised every bit of space, and an awkwardly sloped garden that was transformed into tiers with a scented walkway and plenty of planting. The team also have lots of new gardening tips, including how to plant tall grasses for the best effect, and the boys talk fruit trees and the best way to grow them.
programme.category: Home & Garden, Bouwen en verbouwen, Reality

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Steve Brown showcases some tempting properties to a young couple of keen cyclists who have a budget of £600,000 to make the move from London to the beautiful countryside of North Yorkshire. Steve also meets a truly inspirational, award-winning cheese maker from Syria who's brought a taste of home to the British countryside.
programme.category: Home & Garden, Bouwen en verbouwen, Business & Financial

programme.title: My Unique B&B
programme.desc: In rural Oxfordshire, Simon Parfett and his team meet Asiyla and her family. They live in a beautiful spot where the three generations of her family live and farm together. Among the free-roaming geese sits an old sailing boat, which Asiyla thinks could make unique glamping accommodation with stunning views of the pasture. Once Simon makes it watertight, he works on creating a field kitchen for alfresco cooking, while designer Verity restores the boat's nautical feel.

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: It's the fourth leg of our trip with experts Steven Moore and Angus Ashworth and they get an awful lot closer to home. After clocking up the miles in lovely lowland Scotland their Hillman Super Minx will today be hitting the byways of Derbyshire and Yorkshire. Angus and Steven also visit a couple of venerable survivors from the Industrial Revolution and enjoy yet more delicious indigenous pastry products, before heading off to an exciting auction in the North Yorkshire town of Bedale. But will Angus's crystal ball prophecy prove correct?

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: The quiz in which contestants try to score as few points as possible by plumbing the depths of their general knowledge to come up with the answers no-one else could think of. Presented by Alexander Armstrong and co-host Richard Osman.

programme.title: BBC News at Six
programme.desc: The latest national and international news stories from the BBC News team, followed by weather.

programme.title: Regional News and Weather
programme.desc: The latest news, sport and weather.

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Ronan Keating bring you the stories that matter from across the country.

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Frankie's nerves overcome her, Bobby gets a surprise and wonders if Dana has been honest, and Callum fears the worst when he learns the truth.

programme.title: Eat Well for Less?
programme.desc: Gregg Wallace and Chris Bavin are in Windsor with single mum Holly, who has her hands full looking after her two sons, Spencer, 16, and Fletcher, 11. Caring for Spencer, who has cerebral palsy, leaves Holly little time to cook, and as a result the family have become dependent on microwavable ready meals. Whilst Spencer is happy to eat most foods, Fletcher who has recently been diagnosed with autism, is fussier. He regularly skips meals and, like his mum, relies on sugary snacks to get him through the day. It's time for this family's diet to have a nutritional overhaul. Can Gregg and Chris get the family cooking tasty food from scratch and tempt them away from their favourite big brands in order to save enough for a much-needed holiday?Gregg teaches Holly three simple egg recipes she can quickly whip up for the boys after school, whilst Chris gets Fletcher baking breakfast muffins to set him up for the day and hopefully reduce his snacking. A group from the Wiltshire Wildlife Trust te.

programme.title: Uprising
programme.desc: After the New Cross fire and the Black People's Day of Action, tensions between the community and the police escalated when a massive stop-and-search operation was launched, targeting black people on the streets of Brixton. In April, the situation boiled over into one of the biggest riots in British history. Buildings were burned down and hundreds of police injured. Riots then flared up all over the country, from Southall to Toxteth, but by the year's end, the people of New Cross were no closer to knowing who started the New Cross fire or why - and a lack of answers and justice has lingered over the case ever since.

programme.title: BBC News at Ten
programme.desc: The latest national and international news, with reports from BBC correspondents worldwide.

programme.title: Regional News and Weather
programme.desc: The latest news, sport and weather.

programme.title: The Rap Game UK
programme.desc: The Rap Game UK is back in a new city, Manchester. Six artists from across the UK compete against each other for the chance to win a £20k music prize. In episode one, straight after meeting one another, the artists perform for the first time in front of rap legends Krept, Konan and DJ Target. In a cypher performance all rapping on the same beat, the six must intro themselves - but not all rise to the challenge. Later, after being blown away by the luxury penthouse in the heart of Manchester in which they'll live and work for the duration of the process, the artists discover their next challenge - to write about one day that changed their life. Special guest Russ Millions watches and critiques before the artists discover their first week's rankings, but not all are happy with the result.

programme.title: Newscast
programme.desc: Newsy chat and analysis from Adam Fleming, Laura Kuenssberg, Chris Mason and guests.

programme.title: Our NHS: A Hidden History
programme.desc: David Olusoga meets nurses, doctors and health workers from overseas who have transformed the NHS in spite of hostility and discrimination.

programme.title: Weather for the Week Ahead
programme.desc: Detailed weather forecast.

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: BBC One joins the BBC's rolling news channel for a night of news.

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: The latest news, sport, business and weather from the BBC's Breakfast team.

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones present a lively mix of topical content and expert advice.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A three-bed semi in Darwen in Lancashire, a plot of land in Cromford in Derbyshire and an end terrace in Chatham in Kent are all sold under the hammer. Martin, Dion and Martel talk to the developers and follow progress as they work on their auction buys.
programme.category: Home & Garden, Bouwen en verbouwen, Documentaire

programme.title: BBC News at One
programme.desc: The latest national and international news from the BBC.

programme.title: Regional News and Weather
programme.desc: The latest news, sport and weather.

programme.title: Olympics 2020: Opening Ceremony
programme.desc: Live coverage from Tokyo's National Stadium, where, after a delay of a year, the games of the thirty-second Olympiad will finally be declared open and the Olympic flame will be lit. The global pandemic and the suffering it has caused means that the ceremony will be simpler than had been originally envisaged, but it is sure to still be a spectacular and moving showpiece as Japan welcomes the athletes of the world. Presented by Clare Balding and Alex Scott, with ceremony commentary from Hazel Irvine and Andrew Cotter.

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton guides a couple who want to switch their lives from the bustle of Bristol to the fresh country air of the Wye Valley on a budget of £300,000. While exploring the River Wye, Alistair also discovers the slippery challenges of sustainably sourcing and restocking the European eel.
programme.category: Home & Garden, Bouwen en verbouwen, Business & Financial

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: It's the final leg with our experts and new-found chums Steven Moore and Angus Ashworth. Not to mention their sparkling white 1960 Hillman Super Minx called Betty. Today they're out and about in the north east of England, the extremely circuitous open road having somehow taken hundreds of miles to get there from nearby Melrose in the Scottish Borders. There will be Whitby jet, the sheep of the beautiful North York Moors and even a trip back north to Dundee. Plus, Steven takes up painting in the footsteps of the 'Northern Impressionists' and Angus decides to go for gold and parts with a small fortune. He'll need a good sit-down afterwards though!

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: The quiz in which contestants try to score as few points as possible by plumbing the depths of their general knowledge to come up with the answers no-one else could think of. Presented by Alexander Armstrong and co-host Richard Osman.

programme.title: BBC News at Six
programme.desc: The latest national and international news stories from the BBC News team, followed by weather.

programme.title: Regional News and Weather
programme.desc: The latest news, sport and weather.

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Ronan Keating bring you the stories that matter from across the country.

programme.title: Olympics 2020: Opening Ceremony Highlights
programme.desc: Highlights of the opening ceremony, which took place at Tokyo's National Stadium, signalling the start of the 2020 Olympic Games.

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Tiffany's actions backfire and she is left mortified. Sonia plans to spend some quality time with her dad, and Zack has a decision to make.

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: The team is introduced to the crazy world of rock and roll when the lead singer of a band is murdered on Saint Marie. Can DI Goodman keep control of the unruly suspects for long enough to solve the crime?

programme.title: BBC News at Ten
programme.desc: The latest national and international news, with reports from BBC correspondents worldwide.

programme.title: Regional News and Weather
programme.desc: The latest news, sport and weather.

programme.title: The Lost Boys
programme.desc: When their mother moves them to a small town on the California coast, two teenage brothers discover that a local gang of bikers are all vampires.

programme.title: Olympics 2020
programme.desc: JJ Chalmers presents all the action as the 2020 Olympics finally get under way, with the first medals presented, and early British interest in a multitude of events. We'll see the beginning of the men's cycling road race, while in rowing, it's the heats of the men's coxless four, with Britain hoping for a sixth consecutive gold in the event. And the first gold medal of the games will go to the winner of the women's 10m air rifle.

programme.title: Olympics 2020
programme.desc: The first day of the 2020 Olympics continues with the action including the men's road race, where GB's team is stacked with Grand Tour winners. Presented by Dan Walker and Sam Quek.

programme.title: Olympic Breakfast
programme.desc: Team GB's men's road cyclists go for gold, while Max Whitlock is among the British gymnasts competing for places in both team and individual finals in Tokyo. Britain's four-man road race team in Tokyo includes three Grand Tour champions, including Tour de France winner Geraint Thomas, Vuelta winner Simon Yates and Giro d'Italia winner Tao Geoghegan Hart. Whitlock famously won double Olympic gold in Rio and will be aiming for more medals in Tokyo, with team-mate Joe Fraser, the reigning world champion in parallel bars, also bidding for success. Men's road cycling and gymnastics also feature, and there will be some Olympic history made as the first-ever mixed team archery event is held. Introduced by Dan Walker and Sam Quek.

programme.title: Olympics 2020 Live
programme.desc: Adam Peaty begins his campaign to become the first Briton to retain an Olympic swimming title, while the men's road race champion will be crowned in the shadow of Mount Fuji. Peaty has dominated the men's 100m breaststroke since winning the Olympic title in Rio, and became the first man to break the 57-second barrier at the 2019 World Championships. Earlier this year, he retained his European title, and he will be looking to lay down another marker in today's heats. Elsewhere, Team GB's men's hockey team begin their Olympic campaign against South Africa. Presented by Hazel Irvine and Gabby Logan.

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: The latest national and international news from the BBC.

programme.title: Olympics 2020 Live
programme.desc: Hazel Irvine and Gabby Logan introduce the latest action from the opening day of the Olympics, with medals decided in fencing, judo and taekwondo. Britain's Ashley McKenzie is looking for a medal in the men's -60kg judo, while the men's gymnastics qualifying continues, with Max Whitlock and his GB team-mates looking to book places in the team final and in the individual apparatus finals.

programme.title: Olympics 2020
programme.desc: Jason Mohammad recaps the best of the action from the opening day of the Olympics, including a round-up of the hockey and football. There will also be highlights from 3x3 basketball, which is making its debut at these Games.

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: The latest national and international news from the BBC.

programme.title: Regional News and Weather
programme.desc: The latest news, sport and weather.

programme.title: Take Off with Bradley & Holly
programme.desc: Fasten your seatbelts. Gameshow hosted by Bradley Walsh and Holly Willoughby where members of the public compete to win seats on a plane that will 'Take Off' to a dream holiday destination. Packed full of hilarious games, celebrity surprises, inspirational stories and plenty of in-flight entertainment, this week's lucky winners jet off to star-studded Los Angeles and unforgettable Las Vegas.

programme.title: Olympics 2020: Today at the Games
programme.desc: Clare Balding, Alex Scott and guests look back over the opening day of the Olympics, with reports and reaction on all the big stories. The first medals of the games were awarded in shooting, while the men's cycling road race winner was crowned after a gruelling 234km course that included a stretch on the slopes of Mount Fuji. Max Whitlock and his British team-mates were competing in the men's team gymnastics, while medals were also awarded in archery, fencing, judo, shooting and taekwondo.

programme.title: Michael McIntyre's The Wheel
programme.desc: Michael McIntyre's hit gameshow is back. Three contestants hope to answer their way to a fortune, guided by seven celebrities. Celebrities include Clare Balding, Anton Du Beke, Edith Bowman, Rob Rinder, Lady Leshurr, Dr Xand Van Tulleken and Alex Beresford.

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Jacob struggles to find a safe path as tensions with Tina escalate.

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: The latest national and international news from the BBC.

programme.title: Lady Bird
programme.desc: Critically acclaimed comedy drama about a high school student in early 2000s Sacremento as she navigates the tricky world around her and prepares for college. Upset that her poor family insist on sending her to private school - and with her mother believing she is showing a lack of gratitude - the domestic dramas come thick and fast as she also contends with romance, friendships and studies - all the while dreaming of getting the grades that will allow her to escape to a top university.

programme.title: Olympics 2020 Live
programme.desc: British Olympic champions Adam Peaty and Jade Jones are both in action on day two, which also includes skateboarding, an exciting new addition to the games. Presented by JJ Chalmers. Reigning 100m breaststroke champion Peaty will be aiming to book his place in the final, while other swimming highlights include Japanese star Daiya Seto going for gold at his home games in the 400m individual medley. Meanwhile, Britain's Jade Jones begins her quest to become the first taekwondo fighter to win three Olympic gold medals. Team-mate and fellow world champion Bradly Sinden will also be competing. Two years ago, the Doncaster fighter became the first British man to win gold at the World Championships. Key finals: 0230 Men's 400m IM final 0254 Men's 400m freestyle final 0312 Women's 400m IM final 0342 Women's 4x100m freestyle final 0430-0528 Skateboarding - men's street final.

programme.title: Olympics 2020 Live
programme.desc: Multiple Olympic gold medallists Simone Biles and Jade Jones will be among the stars competing as day two gets into its stride in Tokyo. Double taekwondo champion Olympic Jones will expect to be competing in the semi-finals, where a win would guarantee another medal, while Biles will be in action in the qualifying for the team and individual apparatus finals. Meanwhile, Britain's London 2012 silver medallist Lizzie Deignan will be riding in the women's road race against the stunning backdrop of Mount Fuji. South Korea will be hot favourites in the women's team archery but Britain, led by five-time Olympian Naomi Folkard, will be hoping to get in on the medals after winning bronze at the 2019 World Championships. Coverage introduced by Dan Walker and Sam Quek.

programme.title: Olympic Breakfast
programme.desc: Multiple Olympic gold medallists Simone Biles and Jade Jones are among the stars competing as day two gets into its stride in Tokyo. Double taekwondo champion Olympic Jones will expect to be competing in the semi-finals, where a win would guarantee another medal, while Biles will be in action in the qualifying for the team and individual apparatus finals. Meanwhile, Britain's London 2012 silver medallist Lizzie Deignan will be riding in the women's road race against the stunning backdrop of Mount Fuji. South Korea will be hot favourites in the women's team archery but Britain, led by five-time Olympian Naomi Folkard, will be hoping to get in on the medals after winning bronze at the 2019 World Championships. Coverage introduced by Dan Walker and Sam Quek.

programme.title: Olympics 2020 Live
programme.desc: Britain's Lizzie Deignan is hunting for a medal in the women's road race while US star Katie Ledecky will be in action as the swimming heats continue on day two of the Olympics. Deignan memorably won silver at London 2012 - Team GB's first medal of those games - and the former world champion will be hoping to get on the podium again in Tokyo. Ledecky is already a five-time Olympic champion and is set to be one of the stars of these Games. The 400m freestyle has been one of her favoured events but she will face a strong challenge from rising Australian swimmer Ariarne Titmus, who sensationally beat her at the last World Championships. Presented by Hazel Irvine and Gabby Logan. Key finals: 0500-0930 Cycling: women's road race 0840-0910 Archery - women's team final.

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: The latest national and international news from the BBC.

programme.title: Weather for the Week Ahead
programme.desc: Detailed weather forecast.

programme.title: Olympics 2020 Live
programme.desc: Jade Jones will be hoping to win Olympic gold for the third time in a row while fellow British taekwondo fighter Bradly Sinden will also be aiming to be in the medal hunt. Both Britons are looking to make history with Jones trying to become the first athlete to win three taekwondo golds, while Sinden could become Britain's first male Olympics taekwondo champion. Presented by Hazel Irvine and Gabby Logan.

programme.title: Olympics 2020
programme.desc: Jason Mohammad presents coverage of today's action, including the debut of men's skateboarding, and coverage of the Olympic football and hockey competition. There was also the women's team final, featuring the Great Britain team that won bronze at the last World Championships.

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: The latest national and international news from the BBC.

programme.title: Regional News and Weather
programme.desc: The latest news, sport and weather.

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: As more and more of us yearn for a life in the country, Matt Baker and Charlotte Smith roll up their sleeves on a community farm in Herefordshire. Matt helps to save a bee colony and harvests fruits from their walled garden. Charlotte hand milks Snowdrop, one of the community's dairy cows, before making her own butter. It's the final challenge for Dwayne Fields and his gallant team of adventurers as they set out to climb Wales's biggest mountain, Snowdon. Tom Heap investigates why some plant-based products on supermarket shelves may not be as good for us as we might think.
programme.category: Natuur en milieu, Landbouw, Dieren, Reizen, Outdoor, Nieuws

programme.title: Olympics 2020: Today at the Games
programme.desc: Clare Balding and Alex Scott look back at the main events of day two, in which Jade Jones and Bradly Sinden were hoping to make taekwondo history. British cyclist Lizzie Deignan was competing in the women's road race, while Adam Peaty and Katie Ledecky were among those in action in the swimming pool. Peaty is a hot favourite to retain his title in the 100m breaststroke, while Ledecky is aiming to add to her tally of five Olympics golds amassed in London and Rio.

programme.title: Baptiste
programme.desc: Julien and Zsofia track down the tattooed man but he gives little away. Things escalate when the police receive a hostage video, and Emma takes drastic action for her family.

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: The latest national and international news from the BBC.

programme.title: Regional News and Weather
programme.desc: The latest news, sport and weather.

programme.title: Olympics 2020 Live
programme.desc: Adam Peaty will be expecting to claim gold in the men's 100m breaststroke, and Britain will also have high hopes of a third consecutive gold in the men's triathlon. Peaty has been in dominant form since winning in Rio, and the world champion and world record-holder is aiming to become the first British swimmer to retain an Olympic title. Shortly after the men's breaststroke final, American superstar Katie Ledecky will be looking for the first of a possible five golds at these games in the women's 400m freestyle. Alistair Brownlee has won the last two men's triathlon titles but didn't qualify for Tokyo. Can brother and two-time Olympic medallist Jonny finally claim gold at his third games, or could rising star Alex Yee be the man on the top of the podium? Coverage presented by JJ Chalmers.
